name: Bridgwater Guy Fawkes Carnival
dates: First Saturday after November 5th (Guy Fawkes Night). Next event: Saturday, 7 November 2026 at 6:00 PM.
description: The UK's oldest carnival and one of the largest illuminated carnival processions in Europe. Held annually in Bridgwater, Somerset since 1881, the event commemorates the failed Gunpowder Plot of 1605 (Guy Fawkes Night). The procession features enormous floats covered in tens of thousands of lightbulbs creating elaborate illuminated tableaux on themes ranging from fairy tales to current events. The carnival concludes with the spectacular 'squibbing' finale, where hundreds of participants carry long poles topped with fireworks and parade in a line. The Bridgwater Carnival is part of the wider Somerset Carnival circuit, which also includes carnivals in Bridgwater, Burnham-on-Sea, North Petherton, Shepton Mallet, Wells, Glastonbury, and Weston-super-Mare. The procession route runs through the town centre. Grandstand seats are available; road closures are in effect from late afternoon. The event is organized by the Bridgwater Guy Fawkes Carnival Committee.
parade_route_info: Town centre procession; road closures from late afternoon on the day
